description: Figure 6 presents electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) characterization of Fe–30Mn–8Al–C–xCu low-density steels with 0, 1, and 3 wt% Cu tested in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution at room temperature. Panel (a) shows Nyquist plots where discrete experimental points (black squares for 0 Cu, red circles for 1 Cu, blue triangles for 3 Cu) and solid fitted lines (Fitting-0/1/3 Cu) reveal capacitive semicircles whose diameters shrink markedly as Cu content increases; the inset depicts the equivalent circuit comprising solution resistance (Rs), a constant phase element (CPE), and charge transfer resistance (Rct). Panel (b) presents Bode plots of impedance modulus |Z| and negative phase angle versus frequency, confirming that the 0 Cu alloy maintains the highest impedance and broadest phase-angle peak, whereas the 3 Cu alloy exhibits the lowest values. The systematic decrease in semicircle diameter and impedance modulus with Cu addition indicates deteriorating corrosion resistance, which the study attributes to enhanced microgalvanic coupling between Cu-enriched austenite grain boundaries and the matrix, corroborated by SKPFM and XPS analyses showing higher Volta potential differences and altered passive film chemistry at elevated Cu levels.
